holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is up 10.5% today and 14.4% on the week into triple witching tomorrow, with no dated company catalyst on the board to anchor the move. A vertical move of this size into op-ex frequently sees gamma unwind and mean-reversion the following week, and there's no earnings print to bridge you to the next leg. With only 8 shares the decision is qualitative, but this is the textbook window to lighten on strength rather than chase.
INTC.bull: Momentum + short-covering into op-ex can extend if dealers stay short gamma above current strikes.
INTC.bear: No dated company catalyst supporting the move; post-op-ex gamma unwind often gives back stretched 1-week gains.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalysts on the board for NVDA right now — just triple witching tomorrow, which is a flow/positioning event, not a fundamental one. The 8-share position is modest and the tape is constructive (+2.77% today, +2.69% on the week), so there's no forced action; the real window opens when the next earnings date gets confirmed. Until then, treat strength into Friday's expiry as potentially flow-driven rather than a signal to chase.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not company-specific)
NVDA.bull: Tape is firm, AI capex cycle intact, and dealer hedging into witching can extend upside if gamma flips supportive.
NVDA.bear: Witching often unwinds recent positioning — a +2.77% day going in can give back quickly once expiry flows clear, with no earnings catalyst to backstop.

ORCL.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for ORCL right now — just triple witching tomorrow, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. Price action is flat (+0.43% / +0.17% over 5d) so there's no stretched winner to trim and no washout to add into; the position is essentially in a holding pattern until the next earnings print (typically mid-September for Q1 FY26) reintroduces a real window. Best use of the next session is letting OpEx-driven noise pass and re-evaluating once an earnings date confirms.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not company-specific)
ORCL.bull: OCI/RPO growth and AI-capacity bookings remain the structural driver; consolidation here sets up the next earnings move higher.
ORCL.bear: Capex intensity and margin scrutiny on the AI buildout can compress the multiple if the next print disappoints on FCF.

NOK.read: NOK is down 2.3% today and 4.3% on the week with no company-specific dated catalyst in the queue — just tomorrow's triple witching, which is a tape event, not a fundamental one. Without an earnings date or ex-div on the board, there's no defined window here; the recent drift weak into a derivatives expiry can create noise both ways, so the honest move is to let the next confirmed catalyst define the setup rather than react to options-driven flow.
NOK.bull: Weakness into triple witching on no news could be flow-driven and unwind once expiry clears, with networking/AI-infra narrative still intact.
NOK.bear: -4.3% on the week with no catalyst suggests something is leaking; without an earnings date to reset the story, drift can continue.

NBIS.read: NBIS is +29% in five sessions with no dated company catalyst on the tape — this is a pure momentum extension into tomorrow's triple witching, where positioning unwinds and stretched single names often see the sharpest reversals. With no earnings or ex-div anchor to defend the move, the window favors taking some risk off the table on strength rather than chasing.
NBIS.bull: AI-infra momentum and squeezy float; if dealer flows pin or chase, the trend extends through OpEx.
NBIS.bear: +29% in a week with no dated catalyst into triple witching is exactly the setup that mean-reverts hard once gamma rolls off.

KO.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for KO — no confirmed earnings or ex-div date in the feed, just triple witching tomorrow which is a tape event, not a KO event. Position is small (5 shares) and modestly red (-3.2% over 5d, -0.7% today), so this is a quality defensive holding drifting with the tape rather than reacting to news. Without a dated print or dividend window to anchor to, there's no edge in acting here — wait for the next earnings date or ex-div to be confirmed before defining an add or trim window.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro tape event, not KO-specific)
KO.bull: Defensive staples name pulling back into a quad-witch session; weakness on no news is usually noise in KO.
KO.bear: No dated company catalyst means no clear re-rating trigger; drift can continue if the tape stays heavy post-witching.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a fresh June 2026 IPO with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — there's no company-dated catalyst to lean on, so any add/trim window has to be defined by flow, not fundamentals. The immediate event is tomorrow's triple witching (2026-06-19), which for a brand-new listing with thin float and no listed-options history means amplified intraday moves rather than a directional setup. Best posture is to let the first post-IPO earnings date get confirmed and put a real catalyst on the calendar before sizing decisions.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (first one as a public company; flow event, not fundamental)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity name, post-IPO drift and index/ETF inclusion demand can dominate before the first real print lands.
SPCX.bear: No earnings history, lockup overhang risk, and triple-witching flow can whip a thin new listing in either direction.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
